description: A daily composite value representing the sentiment level for an equity.
Proprietary composite analyst score; bearish if < 5, bullish if > 5, neutral if between -5 and 5
Number of analysts providing an earnings estimate for the stock
Percentage of analysts recommending a buy, calculated as (# of analysts recommending buy) / (# of all analysts)
Percent of analysts who have lowered their price target recently, out of all analysts covering the stock
Standard deviation (dispersion) among analysts' earnings estimates for the next fiscal year
Composite rank (0-100) emphasizing dynamic, short-term analyst sentiment signals; higher values indicate stronger buy signals, lower values indicate sell signals
One-month change in the mean (average) analyst earnings estimate divided by the stock price
Difference between actual reported earnings and expected earnings (consensus), divided by share price
Difference between expected (FY1) earnings and previous four quarters' actual earnings divided by price; flags possible negative inflection
Rank (0-100) emphasizing longer-term, fundamental/value metrics; higher values for buy signal, lower for sell signal
Median expected long-term (3-5 years) growth rate in operating earnings as forecasted by analysts
Net percentage of analysts raising minus lowering earnings estimates, out of total analysts covering the stock
Percentage difference between analysts recommending buy and those recommending sell, divided by total number of analysts
Net percentage of analysts raising minus lowering price targets, divided by total number of analysts
Percentage of analysts recommending a sell, calculated as (# of analysts recommending sell) / (# of all analysts)
Rank (0-100) based on equity-style factors, where higher values reflect a stronger buy signal and lower values a sell signal
Percent of analysts who have increased their price target recently, out of all analysts covering the stock
A weekly composite score reflecting market mood for individual equities.
